$MELI Music is a calculated tool for user acquisition. The festival launched in last year. It was created in partnership with Global Talent Services, a division of $UMG (cc: @BillAckman). The core goal is to lock younger consumers into the MercadoLibre ecosystem. It bridges the gap between a digital app and physical culture.

The strategy works as a closed-loop flywheel. First, it builds a subscription moat. MercadoLibre uses Meli Music to drive its Meli+ program. Subscribers get early access to tickets. They also get exclusive cashback. This adds massive value to the subscription. People are far less likely to cancel Meli+ if it guarantees them access to major cultural events.

Next is the fintech engine. Fans must use the Mercado Pago app to get the best deals, buy tickets, or earn the cashback. This forces people to download the app and fund their accounts. Once the user has the digital wallet, Mercado Libre can sell them high-margin products. They offer personal loans and high-yield savings accounts. The concert ticket is just the bait.

Then, there is the digital expansion. Not everyone can fit into the physical venue. So, the company streams the entire festival live on Mercado Play. This is their free streaming service. This turns a physical event into a massive digital tool. Fans log into the app just to watch the show and stay inside the ecosystem.

There is the advertising revenue too. The event integrates Mercado Ads. MercadoLibre monetizes this large, highly engaged audience. They sell sponsorships and ad space to other brands. The festival basically pays for itself while capturing new users.

This strategy is scaling aggressively. The first shows in 2025 featured Trap and Pop artists like Matuê and Luísa Sonza. They were held at the Mercado Pago Hall for 6.5k people. In January they confirmed a massive expansion. The May 2026 edition is moving to the main field of the Mercado Livre Arena Pacaembu. It will hold over 20k people. It will feature massive Sertanejo stars like Ana Castela and Lauana Prado.

Meli Music is a very public strategy. But it is a brilliant business move. By subsidizing live music, MercadoLibre lowers its customer acquisition costs. It builds intense brand loyalty. It outmaneuvers competitors who only compete on cheap products and fast shipping.

4. $MELI - MercadoLibre

- 10Y CAGR: +47.8%
- Price to Growth: 1.01x https://t.co/iN0S2c0pfw

2. $MELI - MercadoLibre

MercadoLibre للحين هي المنظومة الرقمية المسيطرة بأمريكا اللاتينية، تجمع التجارة الإلكترونية، فنتك، لوجستيات، إعلانات، وتمويل في عجلة قوية. بس مع ذلك السهم نازل تقريباً 21% YTD و 35% خلال آخر ست شهور.

المنصة تخدم أكثر من 83 مليون مشتري نشط، وقاعدين يتوسعون بقوة في كولومبيا وتشيلي والإكوادور وبيرو، وأسواق اختراق التجارة الإلكترونية فيها لسه ببداياته.

شبكتهم اللوجستية الخاصة—اللي توصل تقريباً 75% من الشحنات خلال 48 ساعة—تعتبر من الأسرع بالمنطقة وميزة تنافسية كبيرة.

Mercado Pago صار يخدم أكثر من 70 مليون مستخدم نشط، ومعه إصدار 3 مليون بطاقة ائتمانية جديدة في Q4 لحاله، ومحفظة الائتمان كبرت تقريباً للضعف إلى $12.5 billion، والأصول تحت الإدارة ارتفعت +78% إلى $19 billion.

والإعلانات قاعدة تطلع كمحرّك نمو قوي بعد. إيرادات الإعلانات زادت 67% YoY، وبرضه نسبة الإعلانات من GMV لسه منخفضة نسبياً، يعني فيه مجال صعود كبير على المدى الطويل.

ورغم التحديات الهيكلية بأمريكا اللاتينية (تعقيد اللوجستيات، التضخم، وضعف البنية التحتية)، MercadoLibre عارفة تتعامل معها من أكثر من 20 سنة، ومع كذا تحقق نمو إيرادات ثابت +20% وتزيد حصتها السوقية.

Just finished listening to this awesome interview with Tobi Lütke, the CEO of $SHOP. If you love how $MELI's management is highly reactive and long-term focused, you will enjoy studaying Tobi. He runs Shopify with a very singular, engineering-driven mindset.

Tobi talks about how he almost killed Shopify by trying to act like a traditional corporate executive. He was trusting the process too much and delegating poorly. When COVID hit, he did a massive reset. He reviewed every single project himself, canceled ~60% of them, and turned over his entire executive team. He replaced them with former founders because founders do not settle. They have a natural irritation for bad products. He stopped managing the company and started engineering it.

This is a massive mental model shift. Tobi hates competitive analysis because it just leads to mimicry. You do not create a masterpiece by copying a competitor. Instead, he treats others as rivals. Rivalries inspire you to push your own boundaries. It is exactly like the current battle in Brazil with Shopee fighting MercadoLibre for parcel volume dominance. $MELI thrives because that intense rivalry forces them to innovate from first principles, rather than just copying a playbook. Tobi views e-commerce the exact same way. He demands differentiation.

When Shopify stock dropped 80% a few years ago, Tobi did not panic. He looked at the core incentive issues and restructured compensation completely. Instead of rigid corporate plans, he built an internal system with sliders. Every quarter, employees choose their own mix of cash, RSUs, and stock options. It gives the team full agency. This filters out the corporate climbers and brings in high-agency people who want to build long-term value.

Tobi has absolutely zero nostalgia for his past success. He writes hit pieces on his own past work 🤣. He actively roasts old systems and code so his team feels no emotional attachment to them. The sunk cost fallacy kills corporate growth. Tobi kills the sunk cost fallacy by making sure his team feels free to tear down old ideas to build better ones.

You want to own businesses where the founders treat the company like an active simulation. They constantly tweak the inputs, adapt fast, and obsess over the details. They do not care about industry standards. That is where true long-term compounding lives.
